label: DoorDash
description: Connect to DoorDash Drive and Marketplace APIs to create, track, and manage on-demand deliveries.

form.title: Connect DoorDash
form.subtitle: Authenticate with your DoorDash Developer credentials to manage deliveries via Drive or Marketplace.

methods.label: Developer Key + Signing Secret (JWT)
methods.description: Paste your Developer ID and signing secret from the DoorDash Developer Portal. Anton generates HS256-signed JWTs for each API request — no OAuth flow needed.
methods.how_to:
## DoorDash Developer Key + Signing Secret

DoorDash Drive and Marketplace use short-lived HS256 JWTs for authentication. Anton handles signing automatically — you just supply two credentials from the Developer Portal.

### Steps

1. Go to [developer.doordash.com](https://developer.doordash.com) and sign in.
2. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Drive Portal → Credentials** (for Drive/delivery-as-a-service) or **Marketplace → Credentials** (for Marketplace integrations).
3. Locate your **Developer ID** (also called `developer_id` or `access_key`). Copy it.
4. Under the same credentials page, find or generate your **Signing Secret**. Click **Reveal** or **Generate New Secret** if one doesn't exist.
   - ⚠️ The signing secret is shown only once when first generated. Copy it immediately and store it somewhere safe.
5. Paste both values into the fields below.
6. Select the **Environment** — use **Sandbox** while testing; switch to **Production** when you go live.

### Notes

- Anton constructs a JWT with `iss` = your Developer ID and signs it with your secret using HS256 before each API call.
- If you regenerate the signing secret in the portal, update Anton's stored value or all requests will fail with 401 errors.
- Sandbox and Production use separate credential sets; make sure the environment selector matches the credentials you pasted.
- See [DoorDash authentication docs](https://developer.doordash.com/en-US/docs/drive/reference/authentication/) for the full JWT spec.

fields.name: developer_id
fields.label: Developer ID
fields.description: Found in Drive Portal → Credentials or Marketplace → Credentials. Also referred to as Access Key or Developer ID.

fields.name: signing_secret
fields.label: Signing Secret
fields.description: The secret used to sign HS256 JWTs. Shown once when generated — regenerate in the Developer Portal if lost.

fields.name: key_id
fields.label: Key ID (kid)
fields.description: Optional JWT key ID header. Required by some DoorDash API versions — check your credential details page to see if a Key ID is listed alongside your credentials.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Sandbox for testing; Production for live deliveries. Each environment uses separate credentials.

fields.name: integration_type
fields.label: Integration Type
fields.description: Drive for delivery-as-a-service (white-label); Marketplace for restaurant/merchant listings.
